items.title: Voters Know America Needs More Energy. Solar & Storage Are the Overwhelming Choice.
items.summary:
Skyrocketing energy demand, unpredictable fuel prices, and scorching summer heatwaves are sending U.S. electricity bills through the roof. With just 100 days until the 2026 midterm elections, the window is closing for lawmakers to tackle rising costs before voters render their verdict at the ballot box. New polling released by POLITICO shows   ... [continued]
The post Voters Know America Needs More Energy. Solar & Storage Are the Overwhelming Choice. appeared first on CleanTechnica.

items.title: Heybike Mars 2.0, ALPHA, Horizon e-bikes get up to $800 exclusive savings to new lows, Segway EVs up to 46% off, Aventon, more
items.summary:
We’ve got an EV-focused edition of Green Deals for you today, starting with up to $800 exclusive savings you can score on Heybike’s Mars 2.0 Folding e-bike, ALPHA All-Terrain Mid-Drive e-bike, and Horizon Full-Suspension e-bike – all at new low prices starting from $799, after using our code at checkout. We also have the newly launched Segway Back to School EV Sale with up to 46% savings across e-scooters, e-bikes, and the GoKart Pro 2, with this sale offering the second-ever discount on the GT3 Pro SuperScooter to its $2,500 low. There’s also the all-time low prices on Aventon’s Level 3 Commuter and Pace 4 Cruiser e-bikes, and the EGO Power+ 2,100 PSI Cordless Electric Pressure Washer bundle with a 6.0Ah battery, as well as the Schumacher NACS to J1772 EV charger adapter, a VIOFO two-channel dash cam system, and more. As always, you can find all the hangover deals from this week down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Anker SOLIX Summer Camp Outlet Sale with exclusive bonus savings, and more. 
Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

Transit agencies often reach for hydrogen after starting with real operating constraints and skipping a systems-design step. items.title: Tesla swapped a solar owner’s lease contract for the Book of Enoch
items.summary:
A Tesla solar customer went to pull up their lease agreement in their Tesla account this month and found something other than a contract waiting for them: the Book of Enoch, an ancient apocalyptic text about fallen angels.
Not a scanned copy of their agreement. Not an error page. The full text of a roughly 2,000-year-old religious book, sitting exactly where their legally binding solar lease was supposed to be. Electrek confirmed it.
items.translatedTitle: 特斯拉将太阳能所有者的租赁合同换成了以诺书

The meager front panel I/O is the only major drawback to the design.

items.title: 142 AI data center protests staged in 42 states as public opposition increases — organizers brand 'unaccountable' buildouts as an 'unacceptable infringement on our liberty'
items.summary: Data center projects are facing increasing opposition from surrounding communities, making consent even far scarcer than the chips and power needed to run these facilities. Any developer planning to construct one must now consider the people living around the proposed site and take account for the months or years needed to approve it, if it even gets the green light.

items.title: Spanish operational PV assets stabilize at €596,000/MW following 50% value decline
items.summary:
Spain’s PV asset market is entering a stabilization phase after two years of valuation declines, with operational solar assets averaging €596,000/MW. Investors are increasingly favoring projects with regulated revenues, PPAs, and battery storage potential as merchant assets face pressure from falling capture prices.
The post Spanish operational PV assets stabilize at €596,000/MW following 50% value decline appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: 西班牙运营光伏资产在价值下降50%后稳定在€ 596,000/MW

items.title: ML System presents solar tile for demanding architectural applications
items.summary:
Poland-based ML System has launched PhotonRoof, a BIPV solar tile designed to combine photovoltaic generation with traditional roofing aesthetics. Available in three design lines and multiple finishes, the system targets residential, commercial, and heritage projects requiring high architectural integration.
The post ML System presents solar tile for demanding architectural applications appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: 机器学习系统为苛刻的建筑应用提供太阳能瓷砖

items.title: Sinovoltaics updates solar module manufacturer financial stability ranking
items.summary:
The quality assurance provider reported India-based Waaree Renewable Technologies retained the top position in its Altmann-Z score-based quarterly ranking. Its analysts noted the number of companies with healthier scores remained stable at 14. 
The post Sinovoltaics updates solar module manufacturer financial stability ranking appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: Sinovoltaics更新太阳能组件制造商财务稳定性排名

items.title: Solar is booming in Indiana. Not everyone is happy about it.
items.summary: When the largest solar farm in Indiana came online in early 2025, nearby residents in rural Jasper County were not exactly excited.   “It will mess up their fields,” said long-time resident Gena Carlson, referring to solar panels on farmers’ land. Carlson’s concerns, which were echoed by other locals, highlight the…
items.translatedTitle: 印第安纳州的太阳能正在蓬勃发展。不是每个人都对此感到高兴。

items.title: Fox ESS launches high-voltage battery-inverter solution for C&I solar
items.summary:
Fox ESS’s Power Beast platform integrates H3 Plus hybrid inverters with CQ7 high-voltage battery stacks, featuring LFP chemistry, IP65 protection, up to 98.5% inverter efficiency, and more than 95% battery round-trip efficiency. The system supports PV arrays of up to 250 kW, includes three independent battery inputs, and provides EPS backup with transfer times of less than 10 ms.
The post Fox ESS launches high-voltage battery-inverter solution for C&I solar appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: 福克斯ESS为C&I太阳能推出高压电池逆变器解决方案

Enervenue unveils 150 kWh nickel-hydrogen battery
items.summary:
US-based EnerVenue has launched its Energy Rack, a 150 kWh plug-and-play DC storage system using NASA-derived Aqueous Metal Cell technology. The company claims the metal-hydrogen battery platform offers up to 30,000 cycles, reduced thermal management requirements, and a 30-year design life for utility-scale applications.
The post Enervenue unveils 150 kWh nickel-hydrogen battery appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: Enervenue推出150千瓦时镍氢电池

items.title: Brazil sets compensation rules for wind and solar curtailment
items.summary:
Brazil has introduced a compensation framework for wind and solar generators affected by curtailment, covering eligible grid-related and reliability-driven cuts from 2023 to 2025. The rules aim to reduce legal uncertainty, while excluding energy oversupply-related curtailment and highlighting the need for long-term grid expansion and storage solutions.
The post Brazil sets compensation rules for wind and solar curtailment appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: 巴西制定风能和太阳能减排补偿规则

items.title: Argentina’s distributed solar capacity grew 34.5% in H1
items.summary:
The capacity of self-consumption systems connected to distribution grids increased from 118.6 MW at the end of 2025 to 159.5 MW in June, while the number of users generating and feeding surplus electricity into the grid rose 25.8% to more than 4,700.
The post Argentina’s distributed solar capacity grew 34.5% in H1 appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: 上半年阿根廷分布式太阳能产能增长34.5%

items.title: IEA: Global power demand is surging – and renewables are passing coal
items.summary: Global electricity use is growing again, but renewables are expected to overtake coal as the world’s largest source of electricity this year, according to the International Energy Agency (IEA).
items.translatedTitle: IEA ：全球电力需求激增--可再生能源正在超越煤炭

items.summary: Australia's CER has suspended 21 companies from the Small-scale Renewable Energy Scheme (SRES) during the April to June 2026 quarter.

items.summary: The Clean Energy Investor Group (CEIG) has published a report calling on the Australian government to prioritise consistent and timely implementation of the reformed Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation (EPBC) Act.

items.summary:
